title: Haldane Dirac-Mass Signs Determine The Chern Number
summary: In the Haldane honeycomb model, the two Dirac cones acquire masses m_K = M - 3 sqrt(3) t_2 sin(phi) and m_K prime = M + 3 sqrt(3) t_2 sin(phi); because the two valleys have opposite orientation, the total Chern number is one half of sgn(m_K prime) minus sgn(m_K).
aliases: Haldane valley-mass criterion for the Chern number
assumptions: The low-energy description near the two honeycomb Dirac points is valid.
The system is half filled and the bulk remains gapped.
regime: Low-energy Dirac analysis of the Haldane Chern insulator on the honeycomb lattice.
scope: Model-specific lattice bridge showing how a concrete condensed-matter Hamiltonian realizes the Hall-response / Chern-number family without Landau levels.

source_anchors.notes: Archival low-energy mass criterion identifying the two valley masses.
Archival Chern-number formula showing how the valley mass signs determine the Hall phase.
Witten states that Haldane's perturbation gives masses of the same sign to the Dirac modes and produces the quantum Hall phase.

representation: Concrete Haldane-model bridge from lattice perturbations to the integer Hall Chern number.

math_expressions.notes: Effective Dirac masses at the two valleys from sublattice staggering M and complex next-nearest-neighbor hopping t_2 e^{i\phi}.
Total Chern number from the two valleys, whose kinetic terms have opposite orientation.
Pure Haldane mass with no sublattice staggering gives the unit Chern-insulator phase.

symbols.meaning: complex next-nearest-neighbor hopping that breaks time reversal without net flux per unit cell
first Chern number of the occupied band

step_justification: Linearize the lattice model near K and K prime to identify two massive Dirac cones.
Use the opposite valley orientation to assign opposite geometric signs to the two half-Dirac contributions.
Sum the two valley contributions to obtain the Chern-number criterion in terms of the mass signs.
retrieval_hints: Use when an answer must explain the explicit Haldane-model bridge instead of mentioning Haldane only as a citation.
